worth_it_explanation: Samba TV's Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) data enables demographic and household composition reporting that most ad servers cannot produce on their own. But that reporting is only useful if the delivered composition is read against the intended target, if the team examines which specific segments absorbed off-target delivery rather than stopping at an on-target percentage, and if composition is broken out by placement dimension rather than read only as a campaign average. These checks confirm that the demographic composition report is being used as a diagnostic tool, not just a post-flight summary.
intro: Samba TV measures the demographic and household composition of a campaign's audience using ACR data collected from opt-in smart TVs. When a household's television tunes to a program or an ad, ACR identifies the content and ties the exposure to household-level demographic attributes, including age, gender, household income, and presence of children. That signal is then extended to connected TV (CTV) and digital placements, allowing Samba TV to report what share of the delivered impressions reached each demographic segment and how that composition compares to the intended target. These checks examine whether the delivered composition is evaluated against a planned target, whether the team looks at which segments drove off-target delivery rather than stopping at a single on-target rate, and whether composition is examined at a placement or daypart level so a single skewing source does not hide inside the campaign average.

audit_checks.title: Delivered demographic composition is not compared to the intended target audience
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A Samba TV composition report that shows what share of impressions reached each demographic segment is uninterpretable without a planned target. The practitioner cannot determine whether the campaign over-delivered to the right segments, under-delivered, or missed the target demographic entirely. No benchmark exists to compare the delivered figures against.

educate.base:
Samba TV's demographic composition report shows the share of campaign impressions that reached each demographic segment: age bands, gender, household income tiers, and household composition attributes such as presence of children. The report describes what the campaign delivered. Without a documented target composition, the figures have no benchmark to be read against.

A target demographic composition translates the campaign's audience objective into a measurable expectation before the flight runs. A campaign designed to reach adults aged 25 to 54 with household incomes above $75,000 sets a concrete bar: the composition report should show that a meaningful share of impressions landed in that demographic band. When the Samba TV report comes back showing that a large share of impressions reached adults 55 and older with incomes below $50,000, the gap is immediately visible. Without the planned target, the same figures appear in the report but carry no evaluative meaning.

Samba TV expresses demographic composition as a percentage of impressions, a percentage of unique reach, or both. The percentage-of-impressions view reflects where budget was spent. The percentage-of-reach view reflects which people were actually exposed. Both figures are worth comparing to the planned target, and the two can diverge when a narrower demographic segment receives a disproportionate number of exposures.

Planned targets also allow mid-flight adjustments. When an in-flight Samba TV composition report shows that the age skew is drifting away from the target, a practitioner with a documented benchmark can act on that signal while the campaign is still running. A practitioner without a target cannot distinguish drift from expectation.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The campaign brief or media plan does not specify a planned demographic composition or target audience definition in terms Samba TV can report against (age band, gender, income tier)
  • The Samba TV composition report is reviewed after the campaign ends with no prior expectation of what the composition should look like
  • Post-campaign discussion focuses on whether the on-target rate seems high or low in absolute terms rather than against a documented target
You may want to confirm that planned demographic targets are documented in the campaign brief in the same terms Samba TV uses for its composition report before the flight launches.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer targeting households likely to purchase in a specific category, the Samba TV composition report should show whether impressions reached the income and household composition segments most likely to convert. You may want to confirm the planned demographic target is expressed in terms that map directly to the segments Samba TV reports so the post-flight evaluation has a concrete reference point.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser targeting a specific professional or life-stage demographic, you may want to confirm that the campaign brief specifies the target demographic in the same age, gender, and income terms Samba TV uses in its composition report so the delivered figures can be evaluated against the intended audience rather than in isolation.

educate.learn_more_note: Samba TV demographic composition report documentation is available through the Samba TV account team. Contact your account representative for the measurement methodology guide and report field definitions.

investigate.intro: Review the campaign brief or media plan for a documented demographic target, then compare it to the composition figures in the Samba TV report.

samba_tv.label: Samba TV
steps: Open the Samba TV campaign report and locate the demographic composition section. Note the breakdown by age band, gender, household income, and any other segments the report provides.

Pull the campaign brief or media plan and identify the stated target audience. Confirm whether the target is expressed in the same demographic terms Samba TV reports (e.g., adults 25 to 54, household income $75,000 or more).
Compare the planned target demographic share to the delivered share from the Samba TV report. Note the difference in percentage points for each segment.
If no planned target exists in the brief, record that the campaign launched without a documented demographic benchmark and flag it for the next flight.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether a planned demographic target was documented before the flight and compares the delivered composition from the Samba TV report to that target. A campaign with a documented target and a delivered composition within an agreed tolerance of the target passes. A campaign with no documented target, or one where the delivered composition falls outside the expected range with no explanation on file, is a fail, recorded with the planned target (or its absence) and the delivered composition figures.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether a planned demographic target was documented and the delivered composition from the Samba TV report above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Samba TV demographic composition report was reviewed without a documented planned target to compare the delivered figures against. Without a benchmark, the composition data describes what the campaign delivered but cannot determine whether the delivery matched the intended audience. {{voice_recommend}} documenting the target demographic composition in the campaign brief before each flight, expressed in the same age, gender, and income terms Samba TV uses in its report, so the post-flight evaluation has a concrete reference point and mid-flight adjustments can be made when the composition drifts.

audit_checks.title: Off-target delivery is reviewed only as a single on-target rate without examining which segments received it
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Knowing that 62 percent of impressions were on-target tells the practitioner that 38 percent were off-target. It tells nothing about which demographic segments absorbed that off-target delivery, whether those segments skew consistently in a direction that suggests a fixable problem, or whether one placement or daypart is responsible for the majority of the off-target share. Without that breakdown, the off-target rate is a summary without a corrective path.

educate.base:
Samba TV reports demographic composition as a distribution across segments: the share of impressions or unique reach that landed in each age band, gender, income tier, and household composition category. The on-target rate collapses that distribution into a single number by summing the shares that match the defined target. An on-target rate of 65 percent means that 35 percent of delivery went to households or individuals outside the target definition.

That 35 percent is not a single thing. It is a mix of specific segments, and the mix matters. If the off-target delivery concentrated in adults 55 and older and the campaign was targeting adults 25 to 54, that pattern points toward inventory or daypart choices that skew older. If off-target delivery spread evenly across all non-target segments, the pattern suggests something different. The single on-target rate obscures this distinction.

Examining which segments absorbed off-target delivery makes the composition report actionable. A consistent skew toward a specific off-target segment can be addressed by adjusting which inventory sources, channels, or dayparts receive budget. An on-target rate alone cannot support that kind of adjustment because it carries no information about where the delivery went.

Samba TV's composition breakdown is already in the report. The on-target rate is derived from it. Reviewing the underlying segment distribution rather than only the summary rate is a matter of reading the report more completely rather than adding a new data source.

A few signs this check applies:
  • Post-campaign or in-flight review notes only the overall on-target percentage without referencing the specific segments that made up the off-target share
  • Optimization decisions reference the on-target rate but do not identify which demographic segments to reduce exposure to
  • The campaign has run multiple flights with persistent off-target delivery and no segment-level diagnosis has been applied to identify the source
You may want to review the full composition breakdown by segment alongside the on-target rate so any adjustment decisions are aimed at the specific demographic skew rather than the summary figure.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer whose campaign shows a lower-than-expected on-target rate, the segment breakdown can reveal whether the off-target delivery is reaching a demographic with low purchase intent for the category or one that is simply outside the target definition but still valuable. You may want to review which specific segments account for the off-target share before deciding whether and how to adjust.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser whose Samba TV report shows off-target delivery, reviewing which segments received that delivery can indicate whether the targeting parameters need adjustment or whether specific inventory sources are introducing demographic skew. You may want to confirm the segment breakdown is part of the standard report review rather than looking only at the on-target summary.

educate.learn_more_note: Samba TV demographic composition report documentation is available through the Samba TV account team. Contact your account representative for guidance on reading segment-level composition alongside the on-target rate.

investigate.intro: Review the Samba TV composition report's full segment breakdown and confirm whether post-campaign or in-flight analysis identifies which specific demographic segments account for off-target delivery.

samba_tv.label: Samba TV
steps: Open the Samba TV campaign report and locate the demographic composition breakdown. Confirm whether the report shows delivery by individual segment (age band, gender, income tier) and not only an aggregated on-target percentage.

For any campaign where the on-target rate is below the planned target, note which specific segments account for the largest share of off-target delivery. Record the segment name and its share of total impressions or reach.
Check whether previous optimization notes or post-campaign documentation reference specific off-target segments or only the summary on-target rate.
If the report shows only an on-target rate without segment-level detail, ask the Samba TV account team whether a more detailed composition export is available for the campaign.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether post-campaign or in-flight analysis identifies which specific demographic segments account for off-target delivery, not only the aggregate on-target rate. A campaign where segment-level composition is reviewed and off-target segments are identified by name passes. A campaign where review stops at the on-target summary rate with no segment-level diagnosis is a fail, recorded with the on-target rate found and the segments that remain unexamined.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the on-target rate, the specific segments that account for off-target delivery, and whether segment-level review occurred above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Samba TV demographic composition review stops at the overall on-target rate without examining which specific segments account for off-target delivery. An on-target rate describes how much delivery missed the target; it does not identify where that delivery went or what adjustment could reduce it. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the full segment-level composition breakdown from the Samba TV report alongside the on-target summary, identifying which demographic segments account for the largest share of off-target delivery, and using that segment diagnosis to guide placement or daypart adjustments on subsequent flights.

audit_checks.title: Demographic composition is reviewed only as a campaign average rather than by placement dimension, hiding single sources of skew
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A campaign-level composition average combines delivery across all networks, dayparts, apps, and screens. When one inventory source produces strongly skewed delivery while others perform on-target, the campaign average suppresses the signal. The skew appears manageable at the campaign level but would be visible and correctable if the report were segmented by placement dimension.

educate.base:
Samba TV measures demographic composition across the full campaign, and the campaign-level report is a weighted average of delivery across every network, daypart, app, and screen included in the buy. That average can look acceptable even when one component of the buy is producing heavily skewed delivery. The skewing component's effect is diluted by the other components that are performing on-target.

A campaign that delivers across linear TV, connected TV (CTV) apps, and digital display may show an acceptable overall demographic composition while a specific linear TV network or a specific CTV app is delivering almost entirely to an off-target age group. The campaign average obscures this because the skewing source is one of several components, and its impressions are a minority of total delivery. If the skewing source also carries a disproportionate share of budget, the impact is larger than the campaign-level composition figure suggests.

Reviewing composition by placement dimension means segmenting the Samba TV report by network, daypart, app, or screen type and comparing the composition within each segment to the campaign target. This is the same data as the campaign-level report, reorganized to expose variation across components. A practitioner who reviews composition only at the campaign level cannot identify which component is skewing and cannot make a targeted adjustment.

Daypart-level composition is particularly useful for linear TV components of a campaign. Daytime and late-night dayparts tend to reach different demographic profiles than primetime. If a campaign buys across multiple dayparts without reviewing the composition per daypart, a demographic skew introduced by one daypart is invisible in the campaign average.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The Samba TV report is reviewed at the campaign or advertiser level only, with no breakout by network, daypart, CTV app, or screen type
  • The campaign buy includes both linear TV and digital or CTV components, but composition is reported as a single blended figure
  • Persistent off-target delivery is present in the campaign average but previous optimization decisions did not identify a specific network or daypart as the source
You may want to confirm whether the Samba TV report available for the campaign includes a placement-level or dimension-level composition breakout, and if so, whether it is being reviewed alongside the campaign-level summary.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a campaign across linear TV, CTV apps, and digital placements, a single campaign-level composition figure blends delivery from sources that likely reach different household profiles. You may want to confirm that composition is reviewed by placement type so any adjustment decisions are aimed at the specific source of demographic skew.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser whose Samba TV campaign spans multiple dayparts or networks, reviewing composition only at the campaign level may hide a specific daypart or network that is producing off-target delivery at a rate the average does not reflect. You may want to confirm the report is segmented by at least one placement dimension before evaluating performance.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards (Phase I: Video)

investigate.intro: Confirm whether the Samba TV report for the campaign includes a placement-level or dimension-level composition breakout, and whether that breakout was reviewed alongside the campaign-level summary.

samba_tv.label: Samba TV
steps: Open the Samba TV campaign report and confirm whether demographic composition is available segmented by network, daypart, CTV app, or screen type, in addition to the campaign-level summary.

If a placement-level breakout is available, compare the composition within each segment to the campaign-level average and to the planned target. Note any segment where the composition deviates substantially from the campaign average.

For linear TV components, check whether the report breaks out composition by daypart (e.g., daytime, early fringe, primetime, late night). Note whether any daypart shows a demographic skew that differs from the others.

If the report does not include a placement-level or daypart-level breakout, ask the Samba TV account team whether a more detailed export segmented by placement dimension is available for the campaign.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether demographic composition was reviewed at a placement or daypart level in addition to the campaign average, and whether any placement-level skew was identified. A campaign where at least one placement dimension (network, daypart, app, or screen type) was reviewed and any skewing source was identified passes. A campaign where composition was reviewed only at the campaign average level, with no placement-level breakout examined, is a fail, recorded with the campaign-level on-target rate and the placement dimensions that were not reviewed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether placement-level composition was reviewed and whether any placement-level skew was identified above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Samba TV demographic composition review relies on the campaign-level average without examining composition by placement dimension. A campaign that delivers across multiple networks, dayparts, CTV apps, or screens can show an acceptable overall composition while a single component produces substantially skewed delivery. That component's effect is diluted in the average, so the skew is only visible when the report is segmented by placement. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the Samba TV composition report segmented by at least one placement dimension, such as network, daypart, or screen type, to identify whether a specific source is driving demographic skew that the campaign average conceals, and using that finding to guide budget or targeting adjustments on subsequent flights.
